retrieval.startPrompt: Start a retrieval by typing your query below
retrieval.queryModeError: Only supports the following query modes: {{modes}}
retrieval.queryModePrefixInvalid: Invalid query mode prefix. Use: /<mode> [space] your query

querySettings.queryModeTooltip:
Select the retrieval strategy:
• Naive: Traditional text chunk vector retrieval
• Local: Focus on entity retrieval
• Global: Focus on relationship retrieval
• Hybrid: Local+Global
• Mix: Local+Global+Naive
• Bypass: Skip retrieval, send conversation history and current question to LLM
querySettings.responseFormatTooltip:
Defines the response format. Examples:
• Multiple Paragraphs
• Single Paragraph
• Bullet Points
querySettings.topKTooltip: Number of entities and relations to retrieve. Applicable for non-naive modes.
querySettings.chunkTopKTooltip: Number of text chunks to retrieve, applicable for all modes.
querySettings.maxEntityTokensTooltip: Maximum number of tokens allocated for entity context in unified token control system
querySettings.maxRelationTokensTooltip: Maximum number of tokens allocated for relationship context in unified token control system
querySettings.maxTotalTokensTooltip: Maximum total tokens budget for the entire query context (entities + relations + chunks + system prompt)
querySettings.historyTurnsTooltip: Number of complete conversation turns (user-assistant pairs) to consider in the response context
querySettings.onlyNeedContextTooltip: If True, only returns the retrieved context without generating a response
querySettings.onlyNeedPromptTooltip: If True, only returns the generated prompt without producing a response
querySettings.streamResponseTooltip: If True, enables streaming output for real-time responses
querySettings.userPromptTooltip: Provide additional response requirements to the LLM (unrelated to query content, only for output processing).
querySettings.enableRerankTooltip: Enable reranking for retrieved text chunks. If True but no rerank model is configured, a warning will be issued. Default is True.

apiKeyAlert.title: API Key is required
apiKeyAlert.description: Please enter your API key to access the service

settingsPanel.description: View current DocForge server configuration settings
settingsPanel.editNote: These settings are configured via environment variables or the .env file. Restart the server after making changes.

chunking.title: Chunking
chunking.methodTooltip: Method used to split documents: TOKEN_SIZE, SEMANTIC, or HYBRID
chunking.semanticThresholdTooltip: Percentile threshold for semantic breakpoint detection (0.0-1.0)
chunking.semanticMaxTokensTooltip: Maximum content tokens before falling back to token-based chunking

query.title: Query Defaults
query.topKTooltip: Default number of KG entities/relations to retrieve
query.chunkTopKTooltip: Default number of text chunks to retrieve
query.maxEntityTokensTooltip: Maximum tokens allocated for entity context
query.maxRelationTokensTooltip: Maximum tokens allocated for relation context
query.maxTotalTokensTooltip: Maximum total tokens budget for query context

docProcessingPanel.title: Document Processing
docProcessingPanel.description: Configure how PDFs are extracted, how figures are described, and how text is chunked.
docProcessingPanel.infoNote: Changes apply immediately to new document uploads but reset on server restart. For persistent configuration, update the .env file.

engine.title: Extraction Engine
engine.description: Choose how PDFs are parsed. Docling provides layout-aware extraction with proper table and figure handling.
engine.engineDesc: DEFAULT uses pypdf (text only). DOCLING preserves tables, figures, and structure.
engine.imagesScaleDesc: Scale for extracted figure images. 1.0 = 72 DPI, 2.0 = 144 DPI. Higher = better quality but larger.

vision.title: Vision Model
vision.description: When enabled, figures and charts are sent to a vision LLM for text descriptions that flow into the RAG pipeline.
vision.enabledDesc: Requires Docling engine. Each figure is sent to the vision model for description.
vision.willDescribe: Figures will be described by vision model
vision.maxFiguresDesc: Safety limit on vision API calls per document to control costs.
vision.promptDesc: The text prompt sent alongside each figure image to the vision model.
vision.apiKeyNote: API Key is configured server-side via the VISION_API_KEY environment variable for security. It cannot be changed from the UI.

chunking.title: Chunking
chunking.description: How extracted text is split into chunks before entity extraction and embedding. Semantic chunking works well with Docling output.
chunking.methodDesc: TOKEN_SIZE: fixed-size chunks. SEMANTIC: split by meaning shifts. HYBRID: semantic with token-size fallback.
chunking.chunkSizeDesc: Maximum tokens per chunk (100-10,000). Larger chunks retain more context but cost more per embedding.
chunking.overlapDesc: Tokens shared between adjacent chunks (0-1,000). Must be less than chunk size.
chunking.semanticThresholdDesc: Percentile threshold for semantic breakpoint detection (0.0-1.0). Higher = fewer, larger chunks.
chunking.semanticMinSizeDesc: Minimum tokens per semantic chunk (10-1,000). Prevents overly small fragments.
chunking.semanticMaxTokensDesc: Maximum content tokens before falling back to token-based chunking (1,000-500,000).
chunking.note: TOKEN_SIZE splits by fixed token count. SEMANTIC detects meaning shifts via embedding similarity. HYBRID uses semantic splitting with a token-size safety net for very large documents.

extraction.title: Entity Extraction
extraction.description: How entities and relationships are extracted from chunks. Configure via .env file.

evaluationPanel.title: RAGAS Evaluation
evaluationPanel.description: Evaluate RAG system quality using RAGAS metrics
evaluationPanel.ragasNotInstalled: RAGAS evaluation is not available. Please install the evaluation dependencies.
evaluationPanel.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this evaluation result?
evaluationPanel.noResultsDescription: Run your first RAGAS evaluation to assess your RAG system quality.
evaluationPanel.selectDatasetDescription: Choose an evaluation dataset to test your RAG system

about.title: About DocForge
about.subtitle: Intelligent document analysis platform powered by knowledge graphs
about.description: DocForge transforms complex documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, and more) into structured, searchable knowledge. Using vision-powered AI, it extracts text, tables, figures, and equations — even from scanned or garbled files. It builds a knowledge graph of entities and relationships, enabling hybrid retrieval that combines vector search, graph traversal, and full-text search for accurate, citation-backed answers.

features.multimodalDesc: Extract content from PDFs, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and text formats using Docling with vision model support for figures, tables, and equations. Multi-tier healing for garbled or scanned documents.
features.knowledgeGraphDesc: Automatically extract entities and relationships from documents to build an interactive, explorable knowledge graph.
features.taxonomyDesc: Customizable NER entity types that shape knowledge graph construction. Configure domain-specific categories to improve extraction accuracy and retrieval quality for your use case.
features.hybridRetrievalDesc: Query using multiple strategies: local (entity-focused), global (relationship-focused), hybrid, naive (vector), or mix mode with reranking support.
features.evaluationDesc: Measure RAG system quality with faithfulness, answer relevance, context recall, and context precision metrics.

taxonomy.title: Entity Taxonomy
taxonomy.description: DocForge uses Named Entity Recognition (NER) to extract typed entities from documents during knowledge graph construction. The entity type taxonomy is fully customizable — you can use the built-in defaults or define domain-specific types to improve extraction precision.
taxonomy.defaultDesc: This deployment uses a domain-specific taxonomy optimised for EU structural fund evaluation reports, project assessments, and Romanian policy documents.
taxonomy.domainTitle: EU Cohesion Policy Entity Types (16 domain-specific)
taxonomy.domainDesc: For the Romanian EU Structural Funds analysis scenario, a specialized taxonomy captures the specific concepts that matter: operational programmes, managing authorities, budgets, regulations, and geographic regions.
taxonomy.extensible: The entity taxonomy is configured via the ENTITY_TYPES environment variable or the Settings tab. Changing entity types requires re-indexing documents to rebuild the knowledge graph with the new categories.

tabs.documents: Upload and manage your documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and more). Track processing status and view details.
tabs.docProcessing: Configure extraction engine, vision model settings, and chunking parameters for document processing.
tabs.knowledgeGraph: Explore the interactive knowledge graph of entities and relationships extracted from your documents.
tabs.retrieval: Ask questions and retrieve answers from your document knowledge base with citation support.
tabs.evaluation: Run RAGAS evaluations to measure and improve your RAG system's answer quality.
tabs.api: Browse the REST API documentation for programmatic access to DocForge.
tabs.settings: View server configuration including LLM, embedding, storage, and reranking settings.
